title: Escape the Dark Sector - Review
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Rb2-RE864YY

Daniel (@dungeondive) reviews Escape the Dark Sector, Themeborn's sci-fi follow-up to Escape the Dark Castle — "Dark Castle Advanced." Same glorious black-and-white art and tiny-box convenience (boxed to playing in about four minutes), but a few added mechanisms nudge it from party game toward a gamer's game. Where he prefers Dark Castle with a group, he reckons Dark Sector is the better *solo* experience — the near-perfect solo game.

You escape a hellish space station with two characters, each with a cybernetic implant and a heart-monitor health track. The big additions are ranged combat and ammo: weapons carry ballistic or energy ammo dice with a rate of fire, letting you whittle enemies down before close combat, plus a flank card for an extra round. Chapter cards now progress in difficulty across three acts (level 1→3) rather than mixing freely. He opens with a loving digression on Alfred Bester's The Stars My Destination and recommends the Soundscaper app for sci-fi ambience. Huge game, small box, tons of theme — another total hit he's happy to keep alongside Dark Castle.

----

Daniel prefers Dark Castle with a group but Dark Sector solo, crediting its extra ranged-combat crunch. When a sequel adds complexity to a beloved light game, does that pull you in or push you away?
